Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.util

Class CommonsUtil

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.util.CommonsUtil
public class CommonsUtil extends Object
Small null-and-emptiness helpers shared across the failover core.

"Empty" is defined per type, which matters for scatter/gather where a recovered slice may be a collection, a map, or an array:

  • null → empty.
  • Collection → empty when it has no elements or every element is null (an all-null collection carries no usable data, so it is treated as empty).
  • Map → empty when it has no entries.
  • Object[] → empty only when its length is 0. Unlike collections, an array is not inspected element-by-element, so an array of all nulls is not considered empty.
  • Any other object (e.g. a String, a number, a POJO) → never empty, even the empty string "". Only null makes a non-container value "empty".
Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static boolean
    isNotNullOrEmpty(Object object)
    Inverse of isNullOrEmpty(Object) — true when the value carries usable data.
    static boolean
    isNullOrEmpty(Object obj)
    Returns whether the given value is null or "empty", where emptiness is type-specific (see the class javadoc for the exact rules).

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • CommonsUtil

      public CommonsUtil()

  • Method Details

    • isNotNullOrEmpty

      public static boolean isNotNullOrEmpty(Object object)
      Inverse of isNullOrEmpty(Object) — true when the value carries usable data.
      Parameters:
      object - the value to test (any type, may be null)
      Returns:
      true if object is non-null and not empty by the rules of isNullOrEmpty(Object)

    • isNullOrEmpty

      public static boolean isNullOrEmpty(Object obj)
      Returns whether the given value is null or "empty", where emptiness is type-specific (see the class javadoc for the exact rules).
      Parameters:
      obj - the value to test (any type, may be null)
      Returns:
      true if obj is null; an empty or all-null Collection; an empty Map; or a zero-length Object[]. false otherwise.
